rationale: Both scope clauses define the purpose and application boundary of the respective management system standard.
27701 5.2.1 extends 27001 4.1 by additionally requiring identification of applicable privacy legislation, regulatory obligations, and the PII controller/processor role of the organisation.
27701 5.2.2 extends interested-parties analysis to include PII principals and data protection authorities alongside the stakeholders required by 27001 4.2.
PIMS scope must encompass ISMS scope plus explicitly cover PII processing activities; 27001 4.3 requires only ISMS scope.
27701 requires PIMS to be established as an extension of or integrated with the ISMS, adding privacy-specific processes on top of the information security management system required by 27001 4.4.
Leadership commitment requirements in both clauses are structurally identical; 27701 5.3.1 merely adds privacy-specific policy and objective framing.
Policy must include commitment to privacy and protection of PII in addition to information security objectives required by 27001 5.2.
Both clauses require top management to assign and communicate roles and responsibilities for the respective management system.
27701 extends risk/opportunity identification to cover privacy risks (harms to PII principals) in addition to information security risks required by 27001 6.1.1.
Privacy risk assessment uses the same process framework as 27001 6.1.2 but must additionally consider risks to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.
Risk treatment for PIMS extends 27001 6.1.3 by requiring consideration of privacy controls from 27701 Annexes A and B alongside the 27001 Annex A controls.
PIMS objectives must cover privacy protection goals and be consistent with the privacy policy, extending the information security objectives required by 27001 6.2.
Both clauses require planned and controlled management of changes to the management system; 27701 adds no materially new requirement beyond 27001 6.3.
Resources for PIMS implementation mirror the resource requirements for ISMS in 27001 7.1 with no structural addition.
Competence for PIMS includes privacy-specific skills and knowledge of applicable privacy legislation in addition to information security competence required by 27001 7.2.
Awareness requirements extend 27001 7.3 to include awareness of privacy policy, consequences of PII mishandling, and rights of PII principals.
Communication requirements for PIMS and ISMS are structurally identical; both require determining what, when, with whom, and how to communicate.
Documented information for PIMS includes all 27001 7.5 requirements plus privacy-specific records such as PII processing records, consent records, and PIA results.
Operational planning and control for PIMS extends 27001 8.1 to include planning privacy-specific controls and managing PII processing operations.
PIMS information security risk assessment process aligns with 27001 8.2 but must be conducted with privacy risk perspective included.
Risk treatment for PIMS follows 27001 8.3 process and additionally requires implementation of applicable Annex A/B privacy controls.
Monitoring and measurement extends 27001 9.1 to include privacy performance indicators and metrics for PII protection effectiveness.
Internal audit requirements are structurally identical; 27701 5.7.2 adds that the audit programme must cover PIMS-specific controls.
Management review inputs and outputs mirror 27001 9.3 but must additionally address privacy-specific performance data, regulatory changes, and PII principal feedback.
Nonconformity and corrective action requirements are structurally identical between PIMS and ISMS; no material addition in 27701 5.8.1.
Continual improvement clause is structurally equivalent; both require suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness to be improved over time.
27701 6.2.1 references ISO 27002 5.1 Policies for information security with added guidance that policies should address privacy; functionally co-extensive at this granularity.
Information security roles and responsibilities in 27701 6.3.1 mirror 27001 A.5.2 with privacy officer role added as supplemental guidance.
Segregation of duties guidance in 27701 6.3.2 maps 1:1 to 27001 A.5.3; privacy adds requirement to separate PII collection from individual-level decision-making.
Management responsibilities clause mirrors 27001 A.5.4 with privacy compliance added as a management responsibility.
Contact with authorities in 27701 6.3.4 is equivalent to 27001 A.5.5; adds DPA notification as a specific authority contact.
Contact with special interest groups mirrors 27001 A.5.6; privacy adds engagement with privacy advocacy bodies.
Threat intelligence clause maps directly to 27001 A.5.7; privacy extends to include intelligence on privacy threat actors and PII-targeting threats.
Information security in project management extended by 27701 to mandate privacy-by-design and DPIA integration in project lifecycle, going beyond 27001 A.5.8.
Screening requirements in 27701 6.4.1 mirror 27001 A.6.1; privacy adds screening for roles with access to special-category PII.
Terms and conditions of employment map to 27001 A.6.2; privacy adds contractual obligations regarding PII handling and confidentiality.
Information security awareness and training in 27701 6.4.3 maps to 27001 A.6.3; privacy mandates specific PII handling and GDPR-awareness training.
Disciplinary process for security policy violations mirrors 27001 A.6.4; privacy extends to cover PII policy breaches.
Responsibilities after termination map to 27001 A.6.5; privacy adds return/destruction of PII and ongoing confidentiality obligations.
Confidentiality agreements in 27701 6.4.6 are co-extensive with 27001 A.6.6; privacy adds specific reference to PII confidentiality terms.
Remote working controls in 27701 6.4.7 map to 27001 A.6.7 with added privacy guidance on accessing PII from remote locations.
Information security event reporting maps to 27001 A.6.8; privacy extends to require PII breach identification and notification readiness.
Asset inventory in 27701 6.5.1 extends 27001 A.5.9 to require identification of all assets holding PII including legal basis and purpose for each.
Acceptable use of information and assets maps to 27001 A.5.10 with privacy adding rules for acceptable PII use aligned with stated purposes.
Return of assets on termination mirrors 27001 A.5.11; privacy adds explicit requirement to return or destroy PII-bearing assets.
Information classification in 27701 6.5.4 extends 27001 A.5.12 to require PII/special-category PII classification labels within the taxonomy.
Labelling of information maps to 27001 A.5.13; privacy adds labelling for PII sensitivity levels.
Information transfer controls in 27701 6.5.6 map to 27001 A.5.14 with added privacy requirements for lawful basis verification before PII transfer.
Access control policy in 27701 6.6.1 extends 27001 A.5.15 to require need-to-know enforcement aligned with PII processing purposes.
Identity management requirements in 27701 6.6.2 map to 27001 A.5.16; privacy adds unique identification of processors acting on PII.
Authentication information management maps to 27001 A.5.17; no material privacy addition at this sub-control level.
Access rights provisioning and de-provisioning in 27701 6.6.4 maps to 27001 A.5.18; privacy adds revocation on withdrawal of consent.
Information security in supplier relationships maps to 27001 A.5.19; privacy adds requirement to verify supplier privacy compliance.
Privileged access rights management in 27701 6.6.6 maps to 27001 A.8.2; privacy adds restriction of privileged access to PII-processing systems.
Information access restriction maps to 27001 A.8.3; privacy requires access limited to PII needed for specific stated purposes.
Access to source code maps to 27001 A.8.4; privacy adds control over code that processes PII.
Cryptography policy in 27701 6.7.1 maps to 27001 A.8.24; privacy adds requirement to use encryption for PII at rest and in transit.
Physical security perimeters maps to 27001 A.7.1; privacy adds protection of physical storage of PII records.
Physical entry controls maps to 27001 A.7.2; privacy extends to access to areas containing PII-processing equipment.
Securing offices/rooms/facilities maps to 27001 A.7.3; no material privacy addition beyond information security baseline.
Physical security monitoring maps to 27001 A.7.4 with privacy guidance on CCTV data as PII.
Protection against physical/environmental threats maps to 27001 A.7.5; no material privacy addition.
Working in secure areas maps to 27001 A.7.6; privacy adds requirement to prevent accidental disclosure of PII in shared areas.
Clear desk and clear screen policy maps to 27001 A.7.7; privacy emphasises protection from inadvertent PII exposure.
Equipment siting and protection maps to 27001 A.7.8; no material privacy addition.
Security of assets off-premises maps to 27001 A.7.9; privacy adds requirement to protect PII on portable devices.
Storage media management maps to 27001 A.7.10; privacy requires secure erasure of PII from media before disposal.
Supporting utilities maps to 27001 A.7.11; no material privacy addition.
Cabling security maps to 27001 A.7.12; no material privacy addition.
Equipment maintenance maps to 27001 A.7.13; privacy adds protection against PII leakage during maintenance activities.
Secure disposal/re-use of equipment maps to 27001 A.7.14; privacy requires certified destruction of PII before disposal.
Management of technical vulnerabilities maps to 27001 A.8.8; no material privacy addition at this sub-control level.
Configuration management maps to 27001 A.8.9; privacy adds configuration of privacy-protective settings as default.
Capacity management maps to 27001 A.8.6; no material privacy addition.
Protection against malware maps to 27001 A.8.7; privacy adds protection against malware designed to exfiltrate PII.
Information backup maps to 27001 A.8.13; privacy requires PII in backups to be subject to same protection as primary copies.
Logging maps to 27001 A.8.15; privacy adds requirement to log PII access events for accountability.
Monitoring activities maps to 27001 A.8.16; privacy adds monitoring for unauthorised PII access patterns.
Clock synchronisation maps to 27001 A.8.17; required for accurate timestamping of PII processing records.
Operating procedures maps to 27001 A.5.37; privacy adds procedures specifically for PII handling operations.
Network security management maps to 27001 A.8.20; privacy adds network controls that prevent unauthorised PII flows.
Security of network services maps to 27001 A.8.21; no material privacy addition at this granularity.
Segregation of networks maps to 27001 A.8.22; privacy adds isolation of PII-processing systems.
Secure development policy in 27701 6.11.1 extends 27001 A.8.25 to mandate privacy-by-design and data minimisation principles in development lifecycle.
Application security requirements map to 27001 A.8.26; privacy adds PII-specific security requirements in functional specifications.
Secure system architecture maps to 27001 A.8.27; privacy adds privacy-enhancing technologies as architectural requirements.
Secure coding maps to 27001 A.8.28; privacy adds requirements to avoid hard-coded PII in source code.
Security testing maps to 27001 A.8.29; privacy adds privacy-specific test cases including data minimisation and access control.
Outsourced development maps to 27001 A.8.30; privacy adds contractual privacy clauses for outsourced PII-processing systems.
Separation of development/test/production maps to 27001 A.8.31; privacy adds prohibition on using real PII in test environments.
Change management maps to 27001 A.8.32; privacy adds assessment of privacy impact for system changes.
Test information maps to 27001 A.8.33; privacy requires anonymisation or synthetic data instead of real PII for testing.
Protection of information systems during audit testing maps to 27001 A.8.34; no material privacy addition.
Information security in supplier relationships maps to 27001 A.5.19; privacy adds due diligence on supplier PII processing practices.
Addressing security within supplier agreements in 27701 6.12.2 extends 27001 A.5.20 to require data processing agreements with privacy-specific clauses.
Managing ICT supply chain security maps to 27001 A.5.21; privacy adds assessment of PII protection in ICT supply chain.
Monitoring and review of supplier services maps to 27001 A.5.22; privacy adds monitoring of supplier PII processing compliance.
Information security for use of cloud services maps to 27001 A.5.23; privacy adds cloud DPA and data residency requirements.
Incident management planning extends 27001 A.5.24 to specifically include PII breach detection and notification preparation procedures.
Assessment and decision on security events maps to 27001 A.5.25; privacy adds triage criterion for PII-related breach severity.
Response to security incidents maps to 27001 A.5.26; privacy adds PII breach containment and evidence preservation steps.
Learning from security incidents maps to 27001 A.5.27; privacy adds post-incident PII impact analysis and remediation.
Collection of evidence maps to 27001 A.5.28; privacy adds guidance on handling PII contained in forensic evidence.
Information security continuity maps to 27001 A.5.29; privacy adds continuity of PII protection measures during disruption.
ICT readiness for business continuity maps to 27001 A.5.30; no material privacy addition at this granularity.
Identification of applicable requirements extends 27001 A.5.31 to explicitly include privacy laws, regulations, and contractual privacy obligations.
Intellectual property rights maps to 27001 A.5.32; privacy adds IP considerations for PII-containing datasets and software.
Protection of records maps to 27001 A.5.33; privacy adds specific retention and deletion schedules for PII records.
Privacy and protection of PII maps directly to 27001 A.5.34 Privacy and protection of PII; 27701 6.15.4 expands this into a full management system whereas 27001 A.5.34 is a single control.
Independent review of information security maps to 27001 A.5.35; privacy adds PIMS-scope to the independent review mandate.
Compliance with policies and standards maps to 27001 A.5.36; privacy adds compliance verification for PII-handling procedures.
Technical compliance review in 27701 6.15.7 is related to 27001 A.8.8 vulnerability management; privacy adds technical scans for PII exposure vulnerabilities.
Identifying and documenting PII processing purposes is a specific subset of the PII protection control A.5.34; ISO 27001 does not decompose to this granularity.
Identifying the lawful basis for processing is a privacy-specific sub-requirement; closest 27001 anchor is A.5.34 privacy and protection of PII.
Determining when and how consent is required is a privacy-specific sub-control with no equivalent in 27001; maps as subset of A.5.34.
Obtaining and recording consent is a privacy operational control; 27001 A.5.34 is the closest anchor but 27701 goes substantially further.
Privacy impact assessment (PIA/DPIA) is a privacy-specific risk-assessment tool; relationally closest to compliance checking in A.5.36 but is a distinct privacy process.
Contracts with PII processors are a specific type of supplier agreement; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.20 addressing security in supplier agreements.
Joint controller arrangements have no direct 27001 equivalent; relationally closest to supplier/third-party governance in A.5.19.
Records of PII processing activities (ROPA) are a specific privacy sub-requirement of asset/information inventory; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.9.
Determining and fulfilling obligations to PII principals is a privacy-specific requirement with no direct 27001 control; maps as subset of A.5.34.
Determining information to provide to PII principals (privacy notice) is a privacy-specific transparency obligation; closest anchor is 27001 A.5.34.
Providing information to PII principals (transparency) is a privacy-specific control; no equivalent in 27001 Annex A at this granularity.
Mechanisms to allow PII principals to restrict processing is a data subject rights control; maps as subset of A.5.34.
Withdrawal of consent mechanism is a privacy-specific operational control; no 27001 equivalent; maps as subset of A.5.34.
Access to PII by PII principals (data subject access requests) is a privacy-specific right; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Rectification and erasure of PII is a privacy-specific right of PII principals; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Providing PII to principals in portable format (data portability) is a privacy-specific right; no direct 27001 equivalent.
Handling of PII-related inquiries and complaints is a privacy operational process; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Automated individual decision-making safeguards are a privacy-specific requirement (GDPR Art. 22 type); no 27001 equivalent; maps as subset of A.5.34.
Limiting collection to minimum necessary (data minimisation) is a privacy-by-default sub-requirement; maps as subset of 27001 secure development policy A.8.25.
Limiting processing of PII (purpose limitation at design time) is a privacy-by-design principle; maps as subset of A.8.25 with no exact 27001 equivalent.
Accuracy and quality of PII is a privacy-specific data quality obligation; maps as subset of A.8.25; no direct 27001 equivalent.
Minimising PII via de-identification/anonymisation is a privacy technique; closest 27001 control is A.8.10 information deletion; maps as subset.
Implementing privacy by default settings is a privacy-specific design requirement; relationally related to compliance checking A.5.36 but is a distinct privacy obligation.
Retention and disposal schedules for PII are privacy-specific lifecycle controls; relationally related to asset management A.5.9.
Temporary files and draft data containing PII must be disposed of; maps as subset of information deletion control A.8.10.
Controls over PII transmitted via public networks are related to 27001 A.5.14 information transfer; privacy adds purpose-limitation check for each transmission.
Reviewing and assessing privacy impact of processing components is a design-time privacy review; related to secure development A.8.25 but privacy-specific.
Identifying basis for international PII transfers is a privacy-specific requirement for cross-border data flows; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14 information transfer.
Identifying third countries and applicable transfer mechanisms is a privacy-specific cross-border transfer control; maps as subset of A.5.14.
Records of PII disclosure to third parties (disclosure register) is a privacy-specific accountability control; maps as subset of information transfer A.5.14.
PII processor must operate under documented instructions from the controller; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.20 addressing security in supplier/customer agreements.
Processor must only process PII for customer-specified purposes; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34 privacy and protection of PII.
Prohibition on using PII for marketing/advertising without explicit permission is a privacy-specific restriction; maps as subset of A.5.34.
Processor obligation to inform controller of potentially infringing instructions is related to supplier agreement governance A.5.20 but is a privacy-specific escalation duty.
Processor must ensure all staff processing PII are under confidentiality obligations; maps as subset of 27001 A.6.6 confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements.
Sub-processor engagement by processor requires controller authorisation; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
Processor must assist controller in meeting data subject rights obligations; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34; no processor-specific equivalent in 27001.
Return, transfer, or secure disposal of PII at end of service is a processor-specific privacy obligation; maps as subset of 27001 A.8.10 information deletion.
Processor providing audit/compliance demonstration to controller is related to 27001 A.5.36 compliance with policies; maps as related due to auditor access obligation.
Processor must identify all third-country sub-processor transfers; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14 information transfer.
Processor must identify applicable transfer safeguards for international PII flows; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14.
Records of PII disclosures to third parties by processor are a privacy accountability control; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14.
Annex A control: Identifying and documenting purposes for PII processing is the Annex A expression of clause 7.2.1; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Identifying lawful basis is the Annex A expression of clause 7.2.2; no granular 27001 equivalent beyond A.5.34.
Annex A control: Determining when consent required is the Annex A expression of clause 7.2.3; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Obtaining and recording consent is the Annex A expression of clause 7.2.4; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Privacy impact assessment / DPIA; related to 27001 A.5.36 compliance checking; privacy-specific process not in 27001 Annex A.
Annex A control: Contracts with PII processors; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.20 addressing security in supplier agreements.
Annex A control: Joint controller arrangements; related to 27001 A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
Annex A control: Records of PII processing activities (ROPA); maps as subset of 27001 A.5.9 inventory of information and associated assets.
Annex A control: Determining obligations to PII principals; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34 privacy and protection of PII.
Annex A control: Determining information for PII principals (privacy notice content); maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Providing information to PII principals (transparency); maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Mechanism to restrict processing by PII principals; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Withdrawal of consent mechanism; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34; no direct 27001 equivalent.
Annex A control: Access by PII principals (subject access); maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Rectification and erasure of PII; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Portability of PII (data portability); maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34; no 27001 equivalent.
Annex A control: Handling PII-related inquiries and complaints; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex A control: Automated decision-making safeguards; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34; no 27001 Annex A equivalent.
Annex A control: Data minimisation (limit collection); maps as subset of 27001 A.8.25 secure development policy.
Annex A control: Purpose limitation at processing level; maps as subset of 27001 A.8.25.
Annex A control: Data accuracy and quality obligations; maps as subset of 27001 A.8.25; no direct 27001 equivalent.
Annex A control: De-identification and anonymisation of PII; maps as subset of 27001 A.8.10 information deletion.
Annex A control: Privacy by default settings; related to 27001 A.5.36 compliance with policies; privacy-specific design obligation.
Annex A control: PII retention and disposal schedule; related to 27001 A.5.9 asset inventory and A.8.10 deletion.
Annex A control: Disposal of temporary PII files; maps as subset of 27001 A.8.10 information deletion.
Annex A control: PII transmission controls; related to 27001 A.5.14 information transfer with privacy purpose-limitation addition.
Annex A control: Privacy impact review of processing components; related to 27001 A.8.25 secure development policy.
Annex A control: Identifying basis for international PII transfers; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14 information transfer.
Annex A control: Countries and applicable transfer mechanisms for international PII flows; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14.
Annex A control: Records of PII disclosures to third parties; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14 information transfer.
Annex B control: Customer agreement / documented processing instructions; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.20 supplier agreements.
Annex B control: Organisation's purposes for processing are restricted to controller instructions; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34.
Annex B control: Prohibition on marketing/advertising use of PII; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34; no 27001 equivalent.
Annex B control: Obligation to alert controller to infringing instructions; related to 27001 A.5.20 supplier agreement governance.
Annex B control: Confidentiality commitment for all persons processing PII; maps as subset of 27001 A.6.6 confidentiality agreements.
Annex B control: Sub-processor engagement with controller authorisation; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
Annex B control: Assisting controller with data subject rights obligations; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.34; no direct processor-specific 27001 equivalent.
Annex B control: Return, transfer, or secure disposal of PII at contract end; maps as subset of 27001 A.8.10 information deletion.
Annex B control: Processor providing audit/certification evidence to controller; related to 27001 A.5.36 compliance with policies.
Annex B control: Identifying third-country sub-processor transfer basis; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14 information transfer.
Annex B control: Applicable transfer mechanisms for cross-border PII flows by processor; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14.
Annex B control: Records of PII disclosures to third parties by processor; maps as subset of 27001 A.5.14.
